checklist.entry: Use prepared statements and bind values rather than interpolating untrusted SQL.
D1 supports ordered and anonymous parameters in prepared statements.
Use run, raw, first or all according to the result shape needed.
Append LIMIT 1 when expecting a single row; first does not alter the SQL query.

reference_block.name: kv
reference_block.title: KV
reference_block.statement: Workers KV is a key-value database replicated to Cloudflare's global network. A KV binding must point to a namespace ID, and the binding name must be a valid JavaScript identifier.

checklist.entry: Use R2 bindings in Workers to read, list, write and delete objects.
R2 put stores a value and returns object metadata when successful.
R2 deletes are strongly consistent once the Promise resolves.
A Request body can only be accessed once; clone carefully and avoid memory-heavy duplicate reads.
Use Durable Objects for stateful coordination across clients or requests.
Configure Durable Object bindings with a binding name and class name in Wrangler.
Use SQLite-backed Durable Objects for new classes where the docs and plan support them.
